label: epo-shape:epo-acc-ESPDRequestShape
description: When curating an ESPD Request, which is a document issued by a buyer defining how the exclusion grounds and selection criteria should be addressed in a European Single Procurement Document for a specific procurement, several strict relationships must be established. The request must concern exactly one Procedure, representing the legally defined set of administrative activities conducted to conclude one or more contracts, and this relationship must be provided as a specific identifier URL. Furthermore, the request must specify at least one Procurement Criterion to be used, which must also be linked via a specific identifier URL. Finally, the ESPD Request may refer to a Notice, which is a document published by the buyer about market opportunities and results, and any such reference must similarly be recorded using a specific identifier URL.

label: epo-shape:epo-ParticipationConditionsSummaryShape
description: When curating a Participation Conditions Summary, which serves as an overview of the participation conditions for a procurement, you may include up to one general explanatory text detailing the objective rules and criteria that a tender must fulfill. This description must be provided as a standard text string or a language-specific string. Similarly, the summary can contain at most one explanatory text describing the verification method, specifically the means used to validate these participation conditions, also formatted as a standard or language-tagged text string. Finally, to indicate whether the procurement is reserved for the participation of certain types of organizations, the summary may include a reserved procurement explanation, which must be expressed as a direct link to a standardized concept from a controlled vocabulary.

label: epo-shape:epo-NonDisclosureAgreementTermShape
description: When defining a Non Disclosure Agreement Term, which outlines the conditions and stipulations of a contract ensuring that sensitive information is not shared with outside parties, you may specify whether such an agreement is needed. If this requirement is provided, it must be recorded as a single true or false value indicating if the non-disclosure agreement is required.

label: epo-shape:epo-AuxiliaryPartyShape
description: When defining an Auxiliary Party, which represents the role of an agent who may be mentioned in the information exchanged during a procurement process but does not play an active part in it, the dataset may indicate that this party makes documentation available. If this provision of documentation is recorded, it must be formatted as a specific identifier URL that links directly to a valid Documentation entity. This linked entity must represent a structured collection of records, reports, or supporting materials that provide context or explanation regarding a process, requirement, or decision within the procurement activity.

label: epo-shape:epo-SelectedCandidateListShape
description: When defining a Selected Candidate List, which serves as a formal record of candidates admitted to take part in the award phases of procurements, specific data rules apply. The list may include candidates, each represented by a direct link to a Candidate entity, defined as an agent that has sought an invitation or has been invited to participate in specialized procedures such as restricted procedures, competitive dialogues, or innovation partnerships. Additionally, the list may specify at most one start date to indicate the beginning date of the record. If provided, this start date must be a specific identifier URL pointing to a time interval, representing a temporal entity with a defined extent or duration.

label: epo-shape:epo-cat-EnvironmentalEmissionInformationShape
description: When defining Environmental Emission Information, which details the emissions related to a product, work, or service that are released into the environment, several strict data constraints apply. The record must specify exactly one environmental emission code using a specific identifier URL that links to a standardized concept. It must also include exactly one measure, provided as a direct link to a quantity that indicates the unit used for stating the size. Optionally, the emission information may contain a direct link to at most one abstract item, which represents an item, item request, or deliverable related to a procurement need. It may also include a direct link to at most one transport means, describing a particular vehicle or vessel used for the conveyance of goods or persons. Finally, the record may specify at most one calculation method to describe the formula used for obtaining values, such as monetary values, ranking scores, or criterion weighting; if provided, this method must be formatted as either a standard text string or a language-tagged string.

label: epo-shape:epo-FundShape
description: When defining a Fund, which represents a financial resource used to support a procurement process, you must adhere to specific structural guidelines. In the context of the EU, these funds can be divided into programmes, actions, and projects, such as the European Social Fund or the Connecting Europe Facility, and the funding source may even change between the lot and the contract during an unforeseen emergency. To accurately capture this information, you may provide at most one fund programme to indicate the specific EU programme used for funding, which must be formatted as a specific identifier URL linking to a standardized concept. Furthermore, you may specify at most one resource URL, formatted as a valid web address, to provide a direct link to related online systems, such as portals for accessing procurement documents, submitting tenders, or facilitating communication between the buyer and economic operators.

label: epo-shape:epo-ProcurementProcessInformationShape
description: When defining Procurement Process Information, which captures the temporal unfolding or succession of procurement objects, several specific constraints apply. The record may include a direct link to at most one Lot, representing a qualitative, quantitative, or strategic subdivision of the goods, services, or works to be procured. Similarly, it may provide a specific identifier URL for at most one Procedure, which is a legally defined set of administrative activities conducted to conclude one or more contracts. Furthermore, the process information can specify up to one boolean indicator to state if the competition is terminated, meaning no further contracts will be awarded in this procedure; this can be instantiated in the post-award phase even if no contracts are awarded, provided a prior information notice for competition is signaled. It may also include a single boolean value indicating whether the Dynamic Purchase System is terminated, which can similarly be used in a contract award notice regardless of whether contracts were awarded. Finally, the record may contain at most one boolean indicator specifying whether the procurement object is to be relaunched, a detail that can also be recorded during the post-award phase.

label: epo-shape:epo-ExclusionGroundShape
description: When curating an Exclusion Ground, which represents a criterion describing a legal requirement that an economic operator must meet to be a candidate in the procurement, the dataset may include at most one exclusion ground type. This relation indicates the specific classification of the exclusion ground and must be provided as a specific identifier URL that links directly to a standardized concept.

label: epo-shape:epo-SecurityClearanceTermShape
description: When defining a Security Clearance Term, which establishes the conditions and stipulations regarding the status required for individuals to access classified information, state secrets, or restricted areas following a thorough background check, specific data rules apply. You may indicate whether a security clearance is required by providing a single true or false value. Additionally, you can specify at most one deadline, formatted as a specific date and time, to establish the exact time limit by which the security clearance documentation must be submitted to the buyer.

label: epo-shape:epo-sub-EvidenceInformationShape
description: When curating an Evidence Information record, which provides detailed information about a certificate, specific data rules apply. The record must include exactly one direct link to the underlying Evidence, representing the resource that proves a specific requirement is met and indicating the exact certificate the information refers to. Additionally, the record may optionally provide a single true or false indicator to specify whether the certificate proves that the organization fulfills all the required selection criteria.

label: epo-shape:epo-ContractorShape
description: When defining a Contractor, which represents the role of an agent that has signed a contract with a buyer, specific data constraints apply to its relationships. A contractor record may include a direct link to at most one foreseen subcontractor, indicating that the organization plans to utilize a subcontractor. This distinction is particularly important in result notices to clearly associate the proposed subcontractor with the specific organization rather than the tenderer as a whole. Additionally, the contractor may provide a specific identifier URL to at most one actual subcontractor, which is an agent that has an agreement to perform part or all of the contract obligations. Finally, to enforce the business rule that a contractor must be a winner, the record may include a direct link to at most one winner entity, representing the role of the agent to whom a lot has been awarded.

label: epo-shape:epo-DynamicPurchaseSystemTechniqueShape
description: When defining a Dynamic Purchase System Technique, which is a method that allows the selection of candidates throughout a procedure via qualification criteria followed by individual mini-competitions for the award of purchase contracts, several optional properties may be specified. You may provide at most one DPS scope to explain whether a dynamic purchase system is used and by whom, which must be formatted as a specific identifier URL linking to a concept from the designated dps-usage codelist. To establish the timeframe of the system, you can specify a maximum of one estimated duration, represented as a direct link to a time duration value. If the timeframe cannot be determined, you may instead include a single boolean value indicating that the system has an unknown duration. Similarly, if the system's timeframe is endless, you can provide at most one boolean value to indicate that it has an unlimited duration.

label: epo-shape:epo-ful-ShipmentAgreementShape
description: When defining a Shipment Agreement, which serves as the commercial agreement describing the items being shipped between the purchasing and supplying parties, specific acceptance rules must be followed. The agreement must specify at least one buyer who has accepted the terms, represented by a specific identifier URL linking directly to the Buyer, which is the agent that awards the contract or purchases the items. Furthermore, the record must include at least one seller who has accepted the agreement, also provided as a specific identifier URL pointing to the Seller, defined as the agent legally responsible for transferring the ownership of the procurement results, such as goods, services, or works, to the buyer.

label: epo-shape:epo-TechniqueShape
description: When defining a procurement Technique, which represents a method used for conducting procurement procedures where several techniques can be combined such as an eAuction carried out within a Framework Agreement, specific rules apply to recording its timeframe. You may specify a maximum of one estimated validity period, which must be provided as a direct link to a time interval indicating the expected time range in which the technique is valid. Furthermore, you may define at most one actual validity period, which must also be expressed as a specific identifier URL linking to a time interval that dictates exactly until when the technique is applicable.

label: epo-shape:epo-inv-PaymentCardInformationShape
description: When documenting payment card information, which captures details about the card used for payment contemporaneously with invoice issuance, specific data constraints apply. The record must specify exactly one primary account number, provided as a text string, representing the series of digits embossed on the front of the card. Furthermore, the record may optionally include up to one payment card holder name, also formatted as a text string, which identifies the specific entity officially listed by the financial institution as the holder of the account.

label: epo-shape:epo-AssetShape
description: When defining an Asset, which represents a resource necessary for the provision of a service, you may specify two optional attributes. You can provide at most one predominance value, formatted as text, to indicate the estimated percentage-share of assets provided compared to assets used for activities other than public services. Additionally, you may include at most one significance value, also expressed as text, to define the estimated percentage-share of assets provided relative to the overall assets used to provide services under the contract.

label: epo-shape:epo-BuyerShape
description: When defining a Buyer, which represents the role of an agent or organization such as a contracting authority or international organization that awards a contract or purchases items, several specific data rules apply. The buyer record may include direct links to specific Procurement Service Providers to whom the buyer delegates ancillary purchasing activities, such as providing technical infrastructure, procurement advice, or procedure management. To provide further context about its operations, the buyer can include an activity description, formatted as either a standard or language-tagged text string, which serves to complement its main activity code or describe undefined activities. Additionally, the dataset can specify at most one buyer profile, provided as a website URL, where the buyer publishes general information and details regarding its procurement procedures. Finally, the record may contain a maximum of one true or false indicator stating whether the buyer is a contracting entity; this boolean value is crucial for distinguishing whether the organization operates under the specific rules and thresholds of Directive 2014/25/EU or acts as a standard contracting authority under stricter limits.

label: epo-shape:epo-ord-OrderResponseInformationShape
description: When defining Order Response Information, which captures details about an order response, the record must include exactly one status. This status must be provided as a specific identifier URL that links to a standardized concept representing the current state of the response. Additionally, the record may include information that is particular to an individual line item. When this is the case, it must provide a direct link to an Order Response Line, an entity that details the seller's ability to fulfill a given order line, such as indicating the acceptance or rejection of the item, or making a counter-offer.

label: epo-shape:epo-DirectContractShape
description: When defining a Direct Contract, which represents a contract resulting from a lot award outcome and explicitly excludes framework agreements, you can indicate the specific award it stems from. To establish that the contract is a consequence of a given award, you must link it to a Lot Award Outcome, which captures the result concerning the lot that was attributed by the awarder. This relationship must be provided as a specific identifier URL.

label: epo-shape:epo-FrameworkAgreementShape
description: When defining a Framework Agreement, which establishes an agreement between one or more contracting authorities and one or more economic operators, the record may indicate that the contract is a consequence of a specific award decision. This is captured using the results from lot award outcome property. Any such reference must be formatted as a specific identifier URL providing a direct link to a Lot Award Outcome, which represents the official result concerning the lot that was attributed by the awarder.

label: epo-shape:epo-cat-LineShape
description: When defining a Line, which serves as a component of a document providing specific information, several strict data curation rules apply. Each line must specify exactly one item by providing a specific identifier URL that links directly to an abstract item, representing a procurement need such as an item, item request, or deliverable. Additionally, a line may optionally include a specific identifier URL linking to a single associated document, which represents a set of interrelated business information and metadata conveyed in any medium or form. Finally, the line may contain an optional single entry for additional information to provide supplementary data about the record, which must be formatted as standard text or a language-specific text string.

label: epo-shape:epo-CertificationLabelShape
description: When defining a Certification Label, which represents a set of norms, expectations, standards, and policies relating to areas such as social, ethical, or quality standards, specific curation rules apply. You may specify at most one label type to categorize the certification, such as environmental, quality, or social, and this must be provided as a specific identifier URL pointing to a standardized concept. Additionally, the record can include up to one reference URI, formatted as a standard web address, which serves as a direct link to where the detailed label specifications and underlying policies can be found.

label: epo-shape:epo-ConcessionContractShape
description: For a Concession Contract, defined as an agreement between one or more buyers and economic operators that grants the operators the right to exploit the contract's provisions and potentially receive payments, the data model allows you to specify the award outcome that led to this agreement. If you indicate that the contract is a consequence of a specific award, you must provide a direct link to a Lot Award Outcome record. This linked record represents the official result concerning the lot that was attributed by the awarder.

label: epo-shape:epo-ReviewInformationShape
description: When defining Review Information, which captures details about a review decision or a review request, several specific relationships and references can be established. You may provide at most one direct link to a Review Decision, representing the formal information about the decision, and up to one direct link to a Review Request, which captures the details of the request to review the procedure. To connect this information to the broader document, you can include an element reference formatted as a URI that points directly to the relevant class instance within the current notice. Additionally, you may specify at most one direct link to an identifier that relates the review information to a specific eForms section, ensuring the data is accurately mapped to the corresponding part of the electronic form.

label: epo-shape:epo-not-PreMarketConsultationNoticeShape
description: When curating a Pre Market Consultation Notice, which acts as an announcement of a pre-market consultation rather than a formal call for competition, keep in mind that no procurement documents are available at this stage and the consultation may or may not be followed by a future competition. To properly structure this notice, you may indicate what it prepares for by providing a direct link to a potential Procurement Object, thereby gathering information for the whole or a division of goods, services, or works that might eventually be procured. Furthermore, the notice can refer to involved parties by using a specific identifier URL to link to an Agent In Role, a concept that ties an agent to the specific part they play within the situational context of the procurement process.

label: epo-shape:epo-ord-DeliveryAgreementShape
description: When defining a Delivery Agreement, which establishes the terms applying to the delivery of goods, services, and works—often utilizing Incoterms alongside specific delivery conditions—several data constraints must be observed. The agreement may include at most one general delivery term, expressed as a text string, to detail the specific terms and conditions related to the delivery of goods. It can also specify up to one requested shipping priority, provided as text, which indicates the level of importance or sequence assigned to the delivery. Additionally, the record may contain a single true or false indicator to state whether partial delivery is allowed. Finally, the agreement may specify at most one delivery agreement location by providing a specific identifier URL that links directly to a Location entity, representing the spatial region or named place associated with the delivery.

label: epo-shape:epo-MonetaryValueShape
description: When defining a Monetary Value, which represents a number of monetary units specified using a given unit of currency (keeping in mind that in the pre-award phase of procurement, all monetary values are considered to exclude VAT based on Directive 2014/24/EU), specific data constraints must be followed. The record must specify exactly one amount value, formatted as a decimal number, to capture the numeric value of the amount including any decimals. It must also include exactly one currency, provided as a specific identifier URL linking to a concept that identifies the currency according to a standard code list. Additionally, the record may optionally include a currency code list agency ID to identify the agency that maintains the code list, a currency code list agency name, and a currency code list ID to indicate the concept scheme URI used for the list; all three of these optional details must be expressed as either standard or language-tagged strings.

label: epo-shape:epo-inv-CreditTransferInformationShape
description: When detailing Credit Transfer Information, which captures information about a direct payment of money from one bank account into another, several specific data rules apply. You must specify exactly one payment account identifier to uniquely identify the financial account at a payment service provider to which the payment should be made, and this must be provided as a specific identifier URL linking directly to an Identifier record. Additionally, you may provide at most one payment account name, which designates the name of the account capable of sending and receiving payments, formatted as either a standard text string or a language-specific text string. Finally, the record can include a maximum of one payment service provider identifier to specify the institution where the payment account is located, which must also be expressed as a specific identifier URL pointing to an Identifier record.

label: epo-shape:epo-QualityTargetInformationShape
description: When defining Quality Target Information to capture details about a specific performance goal, the record must satisfy three specific requirements. It must contain exactly one direct link to a Contract Term, which establishes the conditions and stipulations defining particularities of the post-award phase. Additionally, the record requires exactly one specific identifier URL for the quality target code, pointing to a standardized concept that classifies the type of performance goal. Finally, it must include exactly one quality target description providing explanatory text about the performance goal, and this description must be formatted as either a standard text string or a language-tagged string.

label: epo-shape:epo-eva-EvaluatorShape
description: When defining an Evaluator, which represents the role of an agent that assesses a tender submitted for a lot, the dataset must indicate who fulfills this specific function. This is captured by the played by relation, which is restricted to at most a single entry. The agent assigned to this role must be provided as a specific identifier URL that links directly to a Person, strictly defined as an individual real person rather than an imaginary character.

label: epo-shape:epo-ful-ReceiptAdviceLineShape
description: When defining a Receipt Advice Line, which provides details concerning the fulfilment of a Despatch Line, you must provide exactly one direct link to the specific Despatch Line for which it is submitted, representing the fulfilment details of an Order Line. Additionally, the record may optionally include up to one specific identifier URL for various quantity metrics, each linking to a Quantity class that represents a counted number of non-monetary units possibly including fractions. These optional metrics consist of a received quantity to indicate the delivered amount, a rejected quantity to indicate the non-accepted amount, an oversupply quantity to indicate any surplus, and a short quantity to indicate any deficit.

label: epo-shape:epo-LotGroupAwardInformationShape
description: When defining Lot Group Award Information to capture award details related to a given group of lots, you must provide exactly one direct link to the specific Lot Group it describes, which represents a combination of several lots used to conduct a comparative assessment of tenders. Additionally, the record may contain a single optional direct link to an approximate group framework agreement value, indicating the estimated monetary value of the lot group within the framework agreement, as well as a single optional direct link to the group framework agreement maximum value. If either of these framework agreement values is provided, it must be formatted as a specific identifier URL pointing to a Monetary Value record, which expresses the financial amount using a specified unit of currency.

label: epo-shape:epo-CertificateShape
description: When defining a Certificate, which serves as proof of conformance to a defined set of criteria to provide assurance of credibility, trust, and transparency in the procurement process, several specific data constraints apply. The record may include at most one direct link to a Certification Label, which identifies the specific set of norms, expectations, standards, or policies that the certificate addresses. Additionally, it can specify a maximum of one certification number, formatted as either a standard text string or a language-specific string, to serve as the unique identifier of the certificate. Finally, the certificate may provide at most one direct link to a Certifier, indicating the organization acting in the role responsible for verifying conformance and issuing the document.

label: epo-shape:epo-ord-OrderChangeConfirmationShape
description: When defining an Order Change Confirmation, which serves as a document providing information on the acknowledgment of an order change, the record must adhere to three strict relational requirements. First, it must include exactly one direct link to the specific Order Change it responds to, thereby taking action in reaction to the formal request specifying the requested change in goods, services, or works to be delivered in respect to a previous order. Additionally, the confirmation must specify exactly one Buyer using a specific identifier URL, indicating the agent responsible for awarding the contract or purchasing the items. Finally, it must provide exactly one direct link to the Seller, identifying the agent who transfers the ownership of the procurement results to the buyer.

label: epo-shape:epo-ProfessionalSuitabilitySummaryShape
description: When defining a Professional Suitability Summary, which provides an overview of the professional ability required for an economic operator to execute a contract, you may specify up to three optional details. You can provide at most one explanatory text describing the profession itself, formatted as either a standard or language-specific text string. Similarly, you may include at most one standard or language-specific text string to explain the relevant law concerning that profession. Finally, the summary can include at most one true or false indicator to specify whether the execution of the contract is strictly limited to a particular profession.

label: epo-shape:epo-ord-ContractInformationShape
description: When defining Contract Information to capture details about a contract, the record must include exactly one direct link to the specific Contract it concerns. This linked Contract represents the voluntary, deliberate, and legally binding agreement between competent parties and relates to the contractor. Additionally, the contract information may include direct links to specific Order Lines to capture information that is particular to one or multiple order lines. Any referenced Order Line must be provided as a specific identifier URL that details a given unit of goods, services, or works requested in the order.

label: epo-shape:epo-ord-OrderAgreementShape
description: When curating an Order Agreement, which is a document sent from the seller to the buyer specifying the goods, services, or works to be delivered concerning a specific order, the record may incorporate specific line items. Any included order agreement line must be provided as a direct link to a specific identifier URL representing an Order Agreement Line, which contains the detailed information concerning a given unit of goods, services, or works mentioned in the overall agreement.

label: epo-shape:epo-OfferShape
description: When curating an Offer, which serves as a document or quotation providing the monetary value and specific details necessary to fulfill the requirements set out in the procurement documents, the record must make available exactly one piece of evidence. To satisfy this requirement, the offer must include a specific identifier URL that directly links to a single Evidence entity, which acts as a resource proving that a specific procurement requirement has been successfully met.

label: epo-shape:epo-cat-BatchShape
description: When defining a Batch, which represents a production lot in which item instances were produced, the record must include exactly one direct link to an Abstract Item—a gathering class for items, item requests, and deliverables related to a procurement need—to indicate the specific item that is manufactured. You may provide at most one batch ID, which must be a specific identifier URL pointing to an Identifier record assigned to this specific batch of the produced item. Furthermore, the batch may specify up to one manufacture date, formatted as a date and time, indicating the day of production. It can also include at most one best before date, formatted as a standard date, to specify the day until which the quality of the item will remain optimal, and at most one expiry date, also formatted as a standard date, to indicate the last day until which the item can be used.

label: epo-shape:epo-SubcontractingEstimateShape
description: When defining a Subcontracting Estimate, which provides an approximation concerning the foreseen subcontracting, several constraints apply to ensure accurate data capture. You may specify at most one estimated percentage, formatted as a decimal number, to represent the estimated proportion foreseen to be subcontracted. To clarify the status of this ratio, you can include a single true or false value indicating whether the subcontracting percentage is known. Similarly, you may provide at most one subcontracting estimated value to represent the estimated financial value of a single subcontract. This must be provided as a specific identifier URL linking to a Monetary Value record, which specifies the amount in a given currency. To confirm the status of this amount, a single true or false value may be used to indicate whether the subcontracting value is known. Finally, you can describe the subject matter to detail the share of the contract that is to be subcontracted, which may be an aggregate of several subcontracts. This description must be formatted as either a standard text string or a language-specific text string.

label: epo-shape:epo-ProcurementCriterionShape
description: When defining a Procurement Criterion, which establishes a criterion specific to a procurement process, several data rules must be followed. You may provide at most one piece of additional information to offer supplementary data, which must be formatted as either a standard text string or a language-tagged string. The criterion can also be associated with specific limitations by linking to Constraint records; any such constraints must be provided as direct links to the respective limitation entities. If complex weighing methods, such as non-linear weighing or analytic hierarchy processes, are required because standard weighing cannot be expressed per criterion, you may include at most one formula. This formula must also be formatted as a standard text or language-tagged string to capture the mathematical equation or description. Finally, the criterion may specify at most one weight value type, which must be provided as a specific identifier URL pointing to a standardized Concept.

label: epo-shape:epo-ful-DespatchLineShape
description: When defining a Despatch Line, which captures the details concerning the fulfilment of an Order Line, several specific rules apply. You must provide exactly one direct link to a Quantity record that represents the despatched quantity, indicating the counted number of non-monetary units that have been sent. Additionally, the despatch line must include exactly one specific identifier URL linking to the Order Line it refers to, connecting it to the details of the requested unit of goods, services, or works. You may optionally specify a single outstanding quantity, provided as a direct link to a Quantity record that accounts for any non-despatched goods items. If applicable, you can also include at most one text explanation detailing the reason for this outstanding quantity that is still to be despatched.

label: epo-shape:epo-ful-GoodsItemShape
description: When defining a Goods Item, which represents an item including its initial packaging during transportation, specific tracking and fulfillment details must be established. The item must include exactly one direct link to a Despatch Line, which provides details concerning the fulfillment of an order line. Additionally, the Goods Item may specify at most one trace ID, provided as a specific identifier URL, which serves as the identifier used for tracking the item during transport, such as an EPC number used in RFID. Finally, the record may also include direct links to monetary values that represent the declared statistical value of the packaged item.

label: epo-shape:epo-sub-ResponseShape
description: When recording a Response, which serves as an answer given to a question that is part of an ESPD Request, several relationships can be established. To indicate the specific question being replied to, the response can provide a direct link to an Information Requirement, representing the requested data that is to be proven. To support the answer, the response may be reinforced by providing a specific identifier URL to an Evidence record, which acts as a resource proving that the requirement is met. Furthermore, the response can define the timeframe to which it applies by providing a direct link to at most one Time interval, representing the temporal duration of the response's validity.

label: epo-shape:epo-OfferIssuerShape
description: When defining an Offer Issuer, which represents the role of an agent that distributes an offer, any reference to the offer it sends must be formatted as a direct link to a valid Offer record. This linked Offer must represent a document providing the monetary value and the details necessary to fulfill the requirements set out in the procurement documents or request for offer, which in the context of e-procurement also includes quotations.

label: epo-shape:epo-sub-ESPDShape
description: When defining an ESPD, which serves as a self-declaration of an economic operator stating that it does not fall under the exclusion grounds and meets the selection criteria for a specific procurement, several relationships can be established. The ESPD may include a direct link to the Tender it is part of, representing the information submitted by the economic operator to specify its offer regarding a specific lot. It may also provide a direct link to the Organization it offers information about. Furthermore, the ESPD may include a direct link to at most one ESPD Request, which is the document issued by a buyer defining how the exclusion grounds and selection criteria should be addressed. Finally, the ESPD must specify at least one Response by providing a direct link to the specific answer given to a question that is part of the ESPD Request.

label: epo-shape:epo-BusinessShape
description: When defining a Business, which represents a private law company registered in a national registry, several specific attributes may be recorded. You may identify the company's beneficial owners, referring to the natural persons who ultimately own or control the organization or on whose behalf a transaction is conducted. Each beneficial owner must be provided as a specific identifier URL linking to a record of a real, non-imaginary person. Additionally, you may specify at most one business size to categorize the enterprise based on its number of employees and turnover; this must be expressed as a direct link to a standardized concept. Finally, the business record may include a single true or false value to indicate whether it is a listed company. This indicator denotes if the entity is a public company listed on a stock exchange and subject to strict disclosure requirements, a detail often used in eForms when the nationality of the beneficial owner is not provided due to the market's existing transparency standards.

label: epo-shape:epo-AwardCriterionShape
description: When defining an Award Criterion, which describes a requirement that a tender needs to resolve and on which it is evaluated and ranked, several specific data rules apply. You may provide at most one true or false value to indicate whether the award criteria are explicitly provided in the procurement documents. To classify the criterion, you can specify up to one award criterion type, which must be formatted as a direct link to a concept representing the category of the award criterion. Additionally, the record may include at most one fixed value, expressed as a decimal number to represent a constant amount. To clarify this number, you may also provide up to one fixed value type, which must be a specific identifier URL linking to a concept that defines the method for interpreting the fixed value, such as whether it pertains to a total or a unit.

label: epo-shape:epo-QualificationCriterionShape
description: When defining a Qualification Criterion, which represents a standard used during the first stage of a procurement procedure, data curators can specify exactly where the criterion's details are formally documented. This is captured using up to three optional true/false flags: a single flag indicating whether the criterion is specified in the ESPD Request, a single flag denoting if it is specified in the Notice, and a single flag stating whether it is specified in the Procurement Documents. Additionally, the record may include a national criterion to indicate that it contains a sub-criterion specific to a given Member State. This is primarily used for purely national criteria to establish mappings from eCertis and corresponds to a UBL-2.3 sub-tendering criterion. If a national criterion is included, it must be provided as a direct link to another Qualification Criterion.
